turns.content: hey. so Kayla graduates next June and I want to do something big for her. any ideas

That's awesome. You've got a nice runway to plan with it being this far out. When you say big, are you thinking a party, a special gift, a trip, or all of the above? And is there a budget range you're working with?
party for sure. probably a gift too. budget wise I can swing a few thousand if I plan for it. I want her to know im proud of her

A few thousand with this much lead time is very workable. For the party, you could rent a venue or do a big backyard cookout — depends on the vibe Kayla would want. Some ideas for making it special: a photo slideshow of her growing up, a guest book people sign with messages, or hiring a DJ. For the gift, popular ones for graduation are a laptop for college, a nice piece of jewelry, or money toward a car. Do you know what she'd lean toward?
she'd probably want a party with her friends more than anything. I don't know half her friends though so I might need to work with her mom on that

That makes sense. Coordinating on the guest list is smart so nobody important gets left out. You could handle the venue, food, and budget side while her mom helps with the invite list and the social stuff. Splitting it up like that keeps things simple and makes sure Kayla gets what she actually wants.
yeah. I was thinking about renting one of those event spaces. nothing crazy but somewhere with enough room for 30 or 40 people

For 30 to 40 people that's a solid size. A lot of event spaces and community halls can handle that comfortably. Start looking now since June is a popular month for graduations and things book up fast. You'll want to lock in a date as soon as you know the ceremony schedule.
good point. I dont know the exact date yet. probably middle of June but ill find out

Once you have the ceremony date, book the venue for later that same day or the following weekend. Either works. A lot of families do the party the same evening or the Saturday after so out-of-town family can make it to both.
the Saturday after might be better. give everybody time to get there. I got family in Memphis who'd want to come up

Saturday after is the move then. Gives your family time to make the drive and takes the pressure off the ceremony day. You could do an afternoon thing — like 2 to 6 — with food, music, and just a good time. Catering BBQ or doing it yourself would both work for that crowd size.
man I could smoke some ribs myself. save some money and itd be better than any caterer anyway

Can't argue with that. Homemade ribs for your daughter's graduation party is about as good as it gets. If you smoke the meat yourself you could put the savings toward a nicer gift or decorations. Just make sure you've got help with sides and setup so you're not stuck behind a smoker all day and missing the party.
ha yeah good point. I dont want to be sweating over a grill while everyones inside. ill figure out the details. just wanted to start thinking about it early

Starting early is the best thing you can do. You've got eight months — that's plenty of time to nail down the venue, the food plan, and a gift. She's going to love it no matter what because she'll know you put the thought in.
appreciate it brother. ill keep you posted
Sounds good. Congrats to Kayla in advance — that's a big deal. Let me know when you're ready to plan the details.
